search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG185 PCI follow-up cardiac rehabilitation recommendations
site:doh.gov.ae post myocardial infarction follow-up standard
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Manufacturer information is not an exact broad follow-up guideline.
Medicine names, durations, and doses were not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: NG185 is exact for adults after acute coronary syndrome or myocardial infarction, including PCI, but not every PCI indication.
setting_applicability: Direct for discharge, rehabilitation, and post-myocardial-infarction follow-up; acute treatment and procedure care are outside the mapped content.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review, local cardiac-rehabilitation availability, and local antithrombotic and referral governance.
recency_verification: Current official NICE NG185 and NG238 recommendation pages were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG185 and NG238 remain current official NICE guidelines; no newer exact UAE post-PCI follow-up source was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped PCI identity and clinician-reviewed bleeding-risk context without medicine or dose generation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports PCI context and clinician-led bleeding, thromboembolic, cardiovascular, and antithrombotic-risk review without mapping medicine names or doses into generated content.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped post-MI management-plan, blood-pressure, renal-monitoring, and arranged-follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports a clear post-MI management plan, blood-pressure and renal monitoring, and bleeding-risk assessment at follow-up.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped exercise-tolerance, cardiac-rehabilitation, and follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented lifestyle discussion after myocardial infarction without autonomously generating diet, activity, smoking, or weight advice.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed lipid-profile fields in adult cardiovascular prevention.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports full lipid profile, blood pressure, BMI, diabetes status, renal function, liver transaminases, thyroid context, muscle symptoms, and clinician-led specialist assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-led lipid medication review without medicine selection or dose mapping.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented adherence, adverse-effect review, lifestyle and cardiovascular risk discussion, repeat lipid and liver testing, annual medication review, and agreed follow-up without mapping doses.

unresolved_source_gaps: The source is specific to acute-coronary-syndrome and post-myocardial-infarction PCI rather than all PCI.
The catheter-review alias, symptom assertions or negatives, access-site and broad examination fields, ECG, CBC, prior angiogram, generic plans, adherence, and generic referral remain unsupported.
Medication selection, treatment duration, doses, and clinical interpretation remain clinician-stated; qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
